assumptions: The question concerns hedge accounting in consolidated financial statements under IFRS 9.
The intragroup dividend has been recognised as an intragroup receivable/payable, ie as a recognised monetary item.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value hedge as a top-level hedge accounting model for recognised assets or liabilities and components of such items.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedge as a top-level hedge accounting model, including for highly probable forecast transactions and specified risk components.

approaches.label: Hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: The provided context includes the separate hedge accounting model for foreign currency risk arising from net investments in foreign operations, which is another top-level IFRS hedge accounting approach relevant to foreign exchange exposures in consolidated financial statements.
